mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing peak levels, which compresses profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases consistently precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns, contradicting the margin compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate market repricing of future central bank tightening, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms a soft-landing labor market, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero Rank IC for 60-day forward returns across multiple recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above the long-term trend directly increases corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant positive relationship with 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising inflation indicates stagflationary supply shocks, which force rapid portfolio de-risking and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Periods of falling INDPRO and rising CPIAUCSL consistently precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns, invalidating the stagflation risk premium mechanism.
